 {"id":"bd-msta","title":"Evidence tail=0 should skip ledger scan","description":"Avoid reading entire evidence ledger when --tail=0; return empty after validating file exists.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T14:06:53.701444454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T14:15:46.865164370Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T14:15:46.864992305Z","close_reason":"Skip ledger scan for tail=0 and harden evidence tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mt63f","title":"add mounted-path ext4 FIEMAP directory-fd EISDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FIEMAP coverage currently focuses on regular-file success and request-flag semantics, but ffs-core already rejects FIEMAP on directories with IsDirectory/EISDIR. Scope: add an ext4 mounted-path ioctl probe against a directory fd that asserts exact EISDIR when the request reaches userspace, with explicit soft-skip handling if the current kernel/FUSE stack blocks the ioctl before dispatch. Acceptance: fuse_e2e distinguishes directory-fd refusal from transport-level FIEMAP unavailability on ext4.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:10:50.349878007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T23:48:11.891412637Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T23:48:11.888798139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mu0n","title":"Guard stale WAL truncation after checkpoint","description":"PersistentMvccStore::truncate_wal() can currently erase commits appended after the last checkpoint snapshot. Reproducer: checkpoint at commit N, commit N+1, then call truncate_wal(); reopen from checkpoint loses N+1. Fix by rejecting truncation when the checkpoint is stale relative to the current commit sequence, under lock ordering that prevents concurrent commits during the check/truncate window. Add a regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:33:05.824400346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T21:07:41.120433470Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T21:07:41.120162827Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["checkpoint","mvcc","persistence","wal"]}
-{"id":"bd-mxdc9","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for mount-time MVCC WAL recovery","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the mount-time MVCC WAL replay boundary in `ffs-core`.

## Target surface
- `OpenFs::from_device(...)` with `OpenOptions.mvcc_wal_path`
- `init_mvcc_store()` replay path around `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2527-2584`
- Recovery-report exposure via `mvcc_wal_recovery()` tests at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:36632-36820`

## Why this matters
This is the bridge between opaque WAL bytes on disk and visible recovered MVCC state. The current unit tests cover a few hand-authored clean/truncated cases, but fuzzing should stress malformed headers, partial commits, oversized counts, replay policy toggles, and recovery-report determinism.

## Scope
- Add a fuzz target that generates WAL files/bytes plus replay-policy selections and opens through the `ffs-core` mount path.
- Seed from the current unit-test WAL encodings and corrupt-tail examples.
- Assert:
  - no panic / no hang
  - identical input => identical recovery outcome/report class
  - `FailFast` never leaves partially visible versions in the store
  - `TruncateToLastGood` never reports more valid bytes than total bytes
  - `commits_replayed`, `versions_replayed`, and `records_discarded` stay finite and self-consistent

## Acceptance
- New fuzz target registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- Seeds checked in under corpus
- Any crashers promoted to regression tests in `ffs-core`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:50.877676653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:50.877676653Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","fuzz","mvcc","recovery","testing","wal"]}
+{"id":"bd-mxdc9","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for mount-time MVCC WAL recovery","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the mount-time MVCC WAL replay boundary in `ffs-core`.

## Target surface
- `OpenFs::from_device(...)` with `OpenOptions.mvcc_wal_path`
- `init_mvcc_store()` replay path around `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2527-2584`
- Recovery-report exposure via `mvcc_wal_recovery()` tests at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:36632-36820`

## Why this matters
This is the bridge between opaque WAL bytes on disk and visible recovered MVCC state. The current unit tests cover a few hand-authored clean/truncated cases, but fuzzing should stress malformed headers, partial commits, oversized counts, replay policy toggles, and recovery-report determinism.

## Scope
- Add a fuzz target that generates WAL files/bytes plus replay-policy selections and opens through the `ffs-core` mount path.
- Seed from the current unit-test WAL encodings and corrupt-tail examples.
- Assert:
  - no panic / no hang
  - identical input => identical recovery outcome/report class
  - `FailFast` never leaves partially visible versions in the store
  - `TruncateToLastGood` never reports more valid bytes than total bytes
  - `commits_replayed`, `versions_replayed`, and `records_discarded` stay finite and self-consistent

## Acceptance
- New fuzz target registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- Seeds checked in under corpus
- Any crashers promoted to regression tests in `ffs-core`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:50.877676653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:24:47Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:24:47Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","fuzz","mvcc","recovery","testing","wal"]}
 {"id":"bd-mzct","title":"Add unit tests for Ext4Superblock query methods in ffs-ondisk","description":"The ffs-ondisk ext4.rs module has 206 tests but several Ext4Superblock query methods are tested only through the ffs-ext4 facade. Add direct unit tests in ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs for: parse_from_image, has_compat/has_incompat/has_ro_compat, is_64bit, group_desc_size, groups_count, has_metadata_csum, csum_seed, validate_checksum, validate_geometry, validate_v1, feature_diagnostics_v1, group_desc_offset, inode_table_offset, locate_inode, inode_device_offset. These tests complement the facade tests already added in ffs-ext4.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T03:23:28.818125035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T03:35:03.599721597Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T03:35:03.599691791Z","close_reason":"Added 25 unit tests for Ext4Superblock query methods in ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs: has_compat, has_incompat, has_ro_compat, is_64bit, group_desc_size, groups_count, has_metadata_csum, csum_seed, validate_checksum, group_desc_offset, inode_table_offset, parse_from_image. All 293 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ondisk","quality","tests"]}
 {"id":"bd-n3s3","title":"ffs-cli fails to compile due to missing ioctl_trace_path field","description":"In crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs, MountOptions is initialized without the recently added ioctl_trace_path field, causing E0063 compilation errors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:50:51.015872536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:17:04.588931935Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:17:04.586214758Z","close_reason":"Verified: ffs-cli MountOptions initializers include ioctl_trace_path; rch cargo check -p ffs-cli passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-n5l9","title":"Align ffs-core durability autopilot floor with ffs-repair policy","description":"ffs-core::DurabilityAutopilot currently accepts/chooses 1% repair overhead (1.01 multiplier), but ffs-repair::autopilot defaults to a 3%-10% search range. This cross-crate drift lets the public RepairPolicy surface undercut the actual repair policy floor. Raise the ffs-core autopilot minimum candidate/default range to 3%-10%, update public docs/comments, and adjust facade/core tests accordingly. Acceptance: ffs-core/ffs tests reflect [1.03, 1.10] bounds and targeted rch check/test pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:38:13.252142493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:45:28.388823859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:45:28.388554659Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
